title: quercus robur subsp. puberula
intro: Quercus robur subsp. puberula, also known as the downy English oak, is a subspecies of the English oak tree (Quercus robur) characterized by its hairy or downy leaves. The bark and leaves of the Quercus robur subsp. puberula plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and astringent properties. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. Quercus robur subsp. puberula is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
